column2: **Static Application Security Testing.** A "White Box" method that analyzes source code or binaries without executing them.
**Dynamic Application Security Testing.** A "Black Box" method that tests the running application from the outside.
**Interactive Application Security Testing.** Uses agents inside the running app to provide more context than DAST alone.
**Software Composition Analysis.** Identifying and managing vulnerabilities in third-party libraries and dependencies.
A dynamic testing technique that sends **random or malformed inputs** to an application to find crashes or flaws.
A **highly formal and structured** manual code review process with specific roles and six stages.

column1: Name the 6 stages of Fagan Inspection.
column2: **Planning, Overview, Preparation, Inspection, Rework, and Follow-up.**

column1: Who is the "Moderator" in a Fagan Inspection?
column2: The person who **manages the process**, leads the meeting, and ensures the steps are followed.
Testing performed after a change to ensure that **existing functionality still works** correctly.
Testing the **smallest possible component** or function of an application in isolation.
A technique where **small errors (mutants)** are intentionally added to code to test the effectiveness of a test suite.
A test scenario that describes how an attacker might **intentionally subvert** or misuse the system.
A **passive monitoring** technique that records all real user interactions with an application in real-time.
A technique that uses **scripts to simulate** user behavior to monitor application performance and availability.
A condition where an application fails to release memory it no longer needs, leading to **depletion of resources** over time.
Testing performed by **end users** to ensure the system meets business requirements and is ready for use.
Testing that verifies the system is **operationally ready** (backups, disaster recovery, maintainability).
A metric that measures the **percentage of source code** executed during testing.
A metric ensuring that **every possible path** of a control structure (e.g., if/else) is executed.
A fuzzing technique that **modifies (mutates)** existing valid data samples to create test inputs.
A fuzzing technique that **generates data from scratch** based on a defined protocol or format specification.
A static analysis technique that maps the **logical paths** (branches, loops) that code can take.
A static analysis technique that tracks how **data is handled and moved** from inputs to outputs.
The principle that a **small number of modules** usually contain the majority of defects in a system.
A method that tests **subsets of input combinations** to achieve broad coverage with fewer test cases.
